---
author: Your Name
fontsize: 11pt
geometry: margin=1in
title: Distribution Digitizer -- Installation Guide (2025)
---

# Distribution Digitizer -- Installation Guide (2025)

This document provides complete installation instructions for the
**Distribution Digitizer** under **Windows 10/11**.

The application uses:

-   R + Shiny
-   Python (Conda environment)
-   GDAL
-   OpenCV
-   Tesseract OCR

This version reflects the current multi-map architecture (2025).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 📚 Citation

If you use this software, please cite:

**Venkatesh M, Forteva S, Zeuss D (2021)**\
*Distribution digitizer: Software for digitizing species distributions
from analogue maps.*\
Version 0.0.1\
https://github.com/environmentalinformatics-marburg/distribution_digitizer_students

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ✅ 1. Install Required Software

Please install the following:

## ✔ R (4.4.x or higher)

https://cran.r-project.org/

## ✔ RStudio Desktop

https://posit.co/download/rstudio-desktop/

## ✔ Git (optional)

https://git-scm.com/download/win

## ✔ Miniconda (RECOMMENDED)

https://docs.conda.io/en/latest/miniconda.html

⚠ Install Miniconda for All Users.

## ✔ Tesseract OCR

https://github.com/UB-Mannheim/tesseract/wiki

Install English + German language packs.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 📁 2. Download or Clone the Project

## Option A -- Using Git

git clone https://github.com/YourUsername/distribution_digitizer.git

## Option B -- Download ZIP

Download ZIP → Extract → Rename folder to:

distribution_digitizer

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 🐍 3. Create Python Environment (IMPORTANT)

Open Anaconda Prompt and run:

conda create -n distribution_digitizer_env python=3.10\
conda activate distribution_digitizer_env\
conda install gdal numpy\
pip install opencv-python pillow pandas rasterio geopandas shapely fiona
imutils pytesseract

Verify installation:

python -c "import cv2, osgeo, numpy; print('Python environment OK')"

The app expects this exact environment name:

distribution_digitizer_env

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 📦 4. Install Required R Packages

Run in RStudio:

``` r
install.packages(c(
  "shiny",
  "shinydashboard",
  "shinyFiles",
  "shinyalert",
  "shinyjs",
  "DT",
  "magick",
  "grid",
  "reticulate",
  "tesseract",
  "leaflet",
  "terra",
  "sf",
  "jsonlite",
  "stringr",
  "dplyr",
  "tidyr",
  "remotes"
))
```

Optional:

``` r
remotes::install_github("karthik/rdrop2")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 🧠 5. Test Python--R Integration

``` r
library(reticulate)
py_config()
```

Ensure the environment `distribution_digitizer_env` is used.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 📂 6. Required Project Structure

distribution_digitizer/\
├── app/\
│ ├── server/\
│ ├── ui/\
│ ├── functions/\
│ ├── global.R\
│ └── app.R\
├── config/\
├── data/\
│ ├── input/\
│ ├── templates/\
│ └── books/\
├── src/\
│ ├── polygonize/\
│ ├── matching/\
│ └── georeferencing/\
└── start_digitizer.bat

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ▶ 7. Start the Application

## Recommended

Double-click:

start_digitizer.bat

## Manual start

``` r
setwd("D:/distribution_digitizer")
options(shiny.port = 8888, shiny.host = "127.0.0.1")
shiny::runApp("app")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 🐛 Troubleshooting

## Python environment not found

conda env list

## ModuleNotFoundError: cv2

conda activate distribution_digitizer_env\
pip install opencv-python

## GDAL error

conda install gdal

## Tesseract not detected

``` r
Sys.setenv(TESSDATA_PREFIX = "C:/Program Files/Tesseract-OCR/tessdata")
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# 🌐 Optional: Docker / Web Deployment

A lightweight demo version can be deployed via Docker, Render,
HuggingFace Spaces, or GitHub Actions.

Large geospatial processing is recommended locally.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# ℹ More Information

Project homepage:\
https://environmentalinformatics-marburg.github.io/distribution_digitizer_webpage

------------------------------------------------------------------------

End of README.
